title: poa fordeana
intro: Poa fordeana, also known as Forde's meadow-grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poa fordeana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Poa fordeana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices, and its extracts have been studied for their potential applications in modern medicine.
